_note: D5 sprint Task C.12 NEW example (2026-06-04). FAIL-by-design example exercising INV-19 HIGH-severity band (gap_pct ≥ 50%). 10×6 m UK private office, 60 m², ceiling 3000 mm, working plane 750 mm. Single task zone Z1 with em_target_lux=500 (BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5 private_office task entry). Engineer specified 12 × LED_PANEL_600 ultra-low-lumen 2000 lm variant (severely value-engineered substitution from the canonical 4500 lm ontology entry — typical bad spec for a 1×4 retrofit into a shallow ceiling void). Lumen-method achieved 198.4 lx; photometric cascade resolves to 200 lx authoritative. Gap = 500 − 200 = 300 lx; gap_pct = 60.0% — falls in INV-19 HIGH band (gap_pct ≥ 0.50). INV-19 fires FAIL HIGH. INV-1 also fires FAIL HIGH (binary lumen-method gate on the 200 < 500 shortfall). INV-11 fires FAIL HIGH (photometric task_area_compliant=false). All three layered failures cite the SAME root cause (the 2000 lm panel substitution). compliance_summary.compliant=false BY DESIGN. CANONICAL FAIL-BY-DESIGN DISCLOSURE: this example is DELIBERATELY FAIL-by-design to exercise INV-19 HIGH-severity band. Engineer-of-record would either bump to ≥31 panels (lumen-method requires N=31 at 2000 lm) OR replace with 4500 lm panels keeping the 4×3 grid (achieved 558 lx — clears all three INVs simultaneously). Mirrored in output.calculation_summary.assumptions[], output.rationale.sections[], output.calculation_summary.non_compliance_flags[], and reasoning.md.

luminaire.type: 600x600 LED Recessed Panel — ultra-low-lumen 2000 lm value-engineered variant

photometric_ies_paths._source: Synthetic 600x600mm recessed LED panel, ultra-low-lumen 2000lm value-engineered variant (vs 4500lm ontology default), 4000K, opal diffuser. Distribution: Lambertian cos(theta) per CIBSE LG7 §6.2. verification_status: synthetic_reference_C3 — used to demonstrate the INV-19 HIGH per-zone failure cascade for severely under-spec'd panel substitutions.
